title: Once Upon a Time in the West
hero_alt: Once Upon a Time in the West, by Peter de Lory
pullQuote: We look at the West as a place that's inexhaustible — and all those things that we romanticize are still there, only now in sparse amounts. Some of the photographs I'm working on now are like romantic epitaphs.
body: Begun in 1988 and worked through 2004, Once Upon a Time in the West adopted the triptych form in 1989 to hold the American West's mythologies and ideologies in a single frame. Fifty-four pieces, made over sixteen years, that read the West the way Edward Abbey, Wallace Stegner, and Cormac McCarthy wrote it: as a place we keep dreaming even as it disappears.
